critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

Two regulatory concerns raised by the model review committee.

Concern 1 (age/region and community rating): ACA community rating rules permit age and geographic rating area as the only member-facing premium rating factors. However, this model is used for internal cost prediction and reserve setting — not for determining individual premium rates. These rules apply to premium determination, not to internal actuarial models. The concern is a scope error.

Concern 2 (prior-year claims as health status proxy): ACA prohibits health status as an individual premium rating factor. However, this prohibition applies to premium determination. Internal actuarial cost prediction models are not subject to this restriction. The actuarial memo, reviewed by outside counsel, confirms this distinction.

Both concerns are based on misapplication of ACA community rating rules to an internal actuarial function.

Verdict: DEFENSE_WINS.
